title: Oracle Is One Step From Junk—Can It Afford the AI Boom?
excerpt: S&P cut Oracle's credit rating to BBB- on AI spending and OpenAI concentration risk, highlighting a widening financial gap versus Microsoft, Alphabet and Ama...
content_html:
Key Points

  • Interested in Oracle Corporation? Here are five stocks we like better.

  • S&P Global Ratings downgraded Oracle's credit rating to BBB-, citing a widening free cash flow deficit and heavy reliance on OpenAI for revenue.

  • Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon and Oracle are all borrowing heavily to fund AI spending, but Oracle has the weakest balance sheet and no rating cushion left.

  • Oracle's negative free cash flow raises the risk of higher interest costs, fewer buybacks, and difficulty covering debt if AI demand slows.

The past few weeks have seen the bond market start asking a question the stock market has mostly been happy to ignore: Who can actually afford the AI buildout?

On July 9, S&P Global Ratings gave its answer for one of the biggest spenders. It cut Oracle Corp.'s (NYSE: ORCL) long-term credit rating to BBB-, the lowest rung of investment grade and just one notch above junk.

→ Buyback Boom: These 3 Companies Are Betting Billions on Their Own Stocks

The stock has since fallen to around $125, down nearly 30% over the past month and nearly 50% over the past year.

What makes the downgrade worth more than a passing glance is what it reveals about the wider group. Every major AI spender is pouring money into building out their supply capabilities, but they're all doing it from different financial positions, and the gap between them is widening fast.

→ 3 Photonics Companies Making Quantum Tech Possible

The Downgrade Isn't Really About Oracle's Business

S&P's reasoning for the Oracle downgrade was blunt.

The agency admitted it had underestimated the scale of investment required for Oracle's AI ambitions, and now expects the company's free operating cash flow deficit to widen to roughly $42 billion in fiscal 2027, nearly double its earlier forecast.

→ AI Data Centers Need Power, and These 2 Industrials Are Cashing In

The other red flag was customer concentration. S&P estimates that around half of Oracle's $638 billion in remaining performance obligations is tied to OpenAI alone, and it described that single relationship as a key credit risk.

The concern is understandable—Oracle has taken on long-term commitments on facilities and equipment to serve OpenAI's demand, so even if the latter's payments were to slow, Oracle's obligations would still need to be paid in full.

Where Each Spender Actually Sits

So what does that mean for Oracle's peers? Well, if you put the four biggest AI spenders side by side, then the divide becomes obvious.

The top two are Microsoft Corp. (NASDAQ: MSFT), which carries an AAA rating, and Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ: GOOGL), which carries an AA+ rating. Next up is Amazon.com Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN), still at the higher end of the scale at AA, while Oracle sits alone down at BBB-.

content_html_zh:
要点

  • 对甲骨文公司感兴趣？ 这是我们更喜欢的五只股票。
  • 标准普尔全球评级将甲骨文的信用评级下调至 BBB-，理由是自由现金流赤字不断扩大以及收入严重依赖 OpenAI。
  • 微软、Alphabet、亚马逊和甲骨文都在大量借贷来资助人工智能支出，但甲骨文的资产负债表最弱，也没有评级缓冲。
  • 如果人工智能需求放缓，甲骨文的负自由现金流会增加利息成本上升、回购减少以及难以偿还债务的风险。

过去几周，债券市场开始提出一个股票市场乐于忽视的问题：谁能真正负担得起人工智能的建设？

7 月 9 日，标准普尔全球评级给出了对支出最大的公司之一的答案。 它将甲骨文公司（纽约证券交易所股票代码：ORCL）的长期信用评级下调至 BBB-，这是投资级别的最低级别，仅比垃圾级高一级。

→ 回购热潮：这 3 家公司在自己的股票上押注数十亿美元

此后该股跌至125美元左右，过去一个月下跌近30%，过去一年下跌近50%。

这次降级之所以值得，是因为它揭示了更广泛的群体。 每个主要的人工智能支出者都在投入资金来增强他们的供应能力，但他们都是从不同的财务状况来做这件事，而且他们之间的差距正在迅速扩大。

→ 3 家光子学公司使量子技术成为可能

降级实际上与甲骨文的业务无关

标准普尔下调甲骨文评级的理由很直白。

该机构承认，它低估了甲骨文人工智能雄心所需的投资规模，现在预计该公司的自由运营现金流赤字将在 2027 财年扩大至约 420 亿美元，几乎是之前预测的两倍。

→ 人工智能数据中心需要电力，这两个行业正在获利

另一个危险信号是客户集中度。 标准普尔估计，甲骨文 6380 亿美元的剩余履约义务中约有一半仅与 OpenAI 相关，并将这种单一关系描述为关键的信用风险。

这种担忧是可以理解的——甲骨文已经对设施和设备做出了长期承诺，以满足 OpenAI 的需求，因此即使后者的付款速度放缓，甲骨文的债务仍然需要全额支付。

每个消费者实际坐在哪里

那么这对甲骨文的同行意味着什么呢？ 好吧，如果你把四个最大的人工智能支出者放在一起，那么差距就变得很明显了。

排名前两位的分别是拥有 AAA 评级的微软公司（纳斯达克股票代码：MSFT）和拥有 AA+ 评级的 Alphabet Inc.（纳斯达克股票代码：GOOGL）。 接下来是亚马逊公司（纳斯达克股票代码：AMZN），仍处于 AA 级别的高端，而甲骨文则独自坐在 BBB- 级别。
